content.title: Archaeologists discover evidence of prehistoric island settlers

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: In early April 2019, Dr Ceri Shipton and his colleagues from Australian National University became the first archaeologists to explore Obi, one of many tropical islands in Indonesia's Maluku Utara province. The research team's discoveries suggest that the prehistoric people who lived on Obi were adept on both land and sea, hunting in the dense rainforest, foraging on the seashore, and possibly even voyaging between islands.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: The excavations were part of a project to learn more about how people first dispersed from mainland Asia, through the Indonesian archipelago and into the prehistoric continent that once connected Australia and New Guinea. The team's earlier research suggested that the northernmost islands in the group, known as the Wallacean islands, including Obi, would have offered the easiest migration route. It also seemed likely that these islands were crucial 'stepping stones' on humans' island-hopping voyages through this region millennia ago. But to support this idea, they needed archaeological evidence for humans living in this remote area in the ancient past. So, they travelled to Obi to look for sites that might reveal evidence of early occupation.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Just inland from the village of Kelo on Obi's northern coast, Shipton and his colleagues found two caves containing prehistoric rock shelters that were suitable for excavation. With the permission and help of the local people of Kelo, they dug a small test excavation in each shelter. There they found numerous artefacts, including fragments of axes, some dating to about 14,000 years ago. The earliest axes at Kelo were made using clam shells. Axes made from clam shells from roughly the same time had also previously been found elsewhere in this region, including on the nearby island of Gebe to the northeast. As on Gebe, it is highly likely that Obi's axes were used in the construction of canoes, thus allowing these early peoples to maintain connections between communities on neighbouring islands.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: The oldest cultural layers from the Kelo site provided the team with the earliest record for human occupation on Obi, dating back around 18,000 years. At this time the climate was drier and colder than today, and the island's dense rainforests would likely have been much less impenetrable than they are now. Sea levels were about 120 metres lower, meaning Obi was a much larger island, encompassing what is today the separate island of Bisa, as well as several other small islands nearby.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Roughly 11,700 years ago, as the most recent ice age ended, the climate became significantly warmer and wetter, no doubt making Obi's jungle much thicker. According to the researchers, it is no coincidence that around this time the first axes crafted from stone rather than sea shells appear, likely in response to their heavy-duty use for clearing and modification of the increasingly dense rainforest. While stone takes about twice as long to grind into an axe compared to shell, the harder material keeps its sharp edge for longer.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Judging by the bones which the researchers unearthed in the Kelo caves, people living there mainly hunted the Rothschild's cuscus, a possum-like creature that still lives on Obi today. As the forest grew more dense, people probably used axes to clear patches of forest and make hunting easier.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Shipton's team's excavation of the shelters at the Kelo site unearthed a volcanic glass substance called obsidian, which must have been brought over from another island, as there is no known source on Obi. It also revealed particular types of beads, similar to those previously found on islands in southern Wallacea. These finds again support the idea that Obi islanders routinely travelled to other islands.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: The excavations suggest people successfully lived in the two Kelo shelters for about 10,000 years. But then, about 8,000 years ago, both were abandoned. Did the residents leave Obi completely, or move elsewhere on the island? Perhaps the jungle had grown so thick that axes were no longer a match for the dense undergrowth. Perhaps people simply moved to the coast and turned to fishing rather than hunting as a means of survival.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: Whatever the reason for the departure, there is no evidence for use of the Kelo shelters after this time, until about 1,000 years ago, when they were re-occupied by people who owned pottery as well as items made out of gold and silver. It seems likely, in view of Obi's location, that this final phase of occupation also saw the Kelo shelters used by people involved in the historic trade in spices between the Maluku islands and the rest of the world.

groups.desc:
Questions 1-7

Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 1?

In boxes 1-7 on your answer sheet, write
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this

questions.title: Archaeological research had taken place on the island of Obi before the arrival of Ceri Shipton and his colleagues.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“before the arrival of Ceri Shipton and his colleagues”意思是“在Ceri Shipton和他的同事到达之前”，这与原文中的“became the first archaeologists to explore Obi”形成了直接对比。原文明确指出Ceri Shipton和他的同事是第一个在Obi进行考古研究的团队，这说明在他们到达之前没有进行过考古研究。因此正确答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: In early April 2019, Dr Ceri Shipton and his colleagues from Australian National University became the first archaeologists to explore Obi, one of many tropical islands in Indonesia's Maluku Utara province.
central_sentences.translation: 2019年4月初，来自澳大利亚国立大学的Ceri Shipton博士和他的同事成为了第一个探索印度尼西亚北马鲁古省众多热带岛屿之一——Obi岛的考古学家。

target_sen.target_sen: In early April 2019, Dr Ceri Shipton and his colleagues from Australian National University became the first archaeologists to explore Obi, one of many tropical islands in Indonesia's Maluku Utara province.

syn_ary.synonym: became the first archaeologists to explore Obi

target_sen.target_sen: In early April 2019, Dr Ceri Shipton and his colleagues from Australian National University became the first archaeologists to explore Obi, one of many tropical islands in Indonesia's Maluku Utara province.

locating_words.locating_word: before the arrival of Ceri Shipton and his colleagues

questions.title: At the Kelo sites, the researchers found the first clamshell axes ever to be discovered in the region.
questions.analysis: 答案是FALSE，因为题目中的信息（第一批蛤壳斧）与原文中的信息相矛盾。文章提到在Kelo发现的蛤壳斧并不是该地区首次发现的，因为在Gebe岛也发现了类似的斧头。因此，题干陈述的信息在原文中被证实为不正确。

central_sentences.sentence: The earliest axes at Kelo were made using clam shells. Axes made from clam shells from roughly the same time had also previously been found elsewhere in this region, including on the nearby island of Gebe to the northeast.
central_sentences.translation: Kelo最早的斧头是用蛤壳制成的。大约在同一时间，蛤壳制成的斧头也在该地区的其他地方被发现，包括东北方向的Gebe岛。

target_sen.target_sen: The earliest axes at Kelo were made using clam shells.
Axes made from clam shells from roughly the same time had also previously been found elsewhere in this region, including on the nearby island of Gebe to the northeast.
Axes made from clam shells from roughly the same time had also previously been found elsewhere in this region, including on the nearby island of Gebe to the northeast.

syn_ary.synonym: including on the nearby island of Gebe

target_sen.target_sen: Axes made from clam shells from roughly the same time had also previously been found elsewhere in this region, including on the nearby island of Gebe to the northeast.

questions.title: The size of Obi today is less than it was 18,000 years ago.
questions.analysis: 根据文章中提到的海平面的变化，“18,000 years ago”当时的“larger”意味着过去的奥比岛比今日的岛屿大，这与题干中的描述 "The size of Obi today is less than it was" 直接对应。因此，这个答案是正确的。

central_sentences.sentence: Sea levels were about 120 metres lower, meaning Obi was a much larger island, encompassing what is today the separate island of Bisa, as well as several other small islands nearby.
central_sentences.translation: 海平面比现在低大约120米，这意味着奥比岛当时是一个更大的岛屿，包括了今天的比萨岛以及其他一些附近的小岛。

target_sen.target_sen: Sea levels were about 120 metres lower, meaning Obi was a much larger island, encompassing what is today the separate island of Bisa, as well as several other small islands nearby.
At this time the climate was drier and colder than today, and the island's dense rainforests would likely have been much less impenetrable than they are now.
Sea levels were about 120 metres lower, meaning Obi was a much larger island, encompassing what is today the separate island of Bisa, as well as several other small islands nearby.
The oldest cultural layers from the Kelo site provided the team with the earliest record for human occupation on Obi, dating back around 18,000 years.

questions.title: A change in the climate around 11,700 years ago had a greater impact on Obi than on the surrounding islands.
questions.analysis: 题干中所述信息提到气候变化对Obi岛和周围岛屿的影响情况。而在原文中，仅提到气候变化对Obi岛的影响，却没有提供任何关于周围岛屿受到影响的信息。因此，无法判断气候变化是否对Obi岛的影响更大，这道题的正确答案应该是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: Roughly 11,700 years ago, as the most recent ice age ended, the climate became significantly warmer and wetter, no doubt making Obi's jungle much thicker.
central_sentences.translation: 大约在11,700年前，最近一次冰期结束时，气候变得明显温暖和潮湿，使Obi岛的丛林变得更加茂密。

target_sen.target_sen: Roughly 11,700 years ago, as the most recent ice age ended, the climate became significantly warmer and wetter, no doubt making Obi's jungle much thicker.
Roughly 11,700 years ago, as the most recent ice age ended, the climate became significantly warmer and wetter, no doubt making Obi's jungle much thicker.
Roughly 11,700 years ago, as the most recent ice age ended, the climate became significantly warmer and wetter, no doubt making Obi's jungle much thicker.
Roughly 11,700 years ago, as the most recent ice age ended, the climate became significantly warmer and wetter, no doubt making Obi's jungle much thicker.

questions.title: The researchers believe there is a connection between warmer, wetter weather and a change in the material used to make axes.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息在原文中可以找到明确的对应表达。题干提到研究者相信气候变得更温暖潮湿与制作斧头材料的变化之间有联系。原文中提到气候变得温暖潮湿，并且研究者认为石斧的出现与此几乎是同时发生的，这表明了两者之间的联系。因此，答案是 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: Roughly 11,700 years ago, as the most recent ice age ended, the climate became significantly warmer and wetter, no doubt making Obi's jungle much thicker. According to the researchers, it is no coincidence that around this time the first axes crafted from stone rather than sea shells appear, likely in response to their heavy-duty use for clearing and modification of the increasingly dense rainforest.
central_sentences.translation: 大约11700年前，最近的一次冰河时期结束时，气候显著变得温暖潮湿，无疑使Obi的丛林变得更浓密。根据研究者们的说法，石斧而非海贝壳斧首次出现的时间与此几乎是同时发生的，这可能是因为需要用更坚固的工具来清理和改造愈加浓密的雨林。

target_sen.target_sen: Roughly 11,700 years ago, as the most recent ice age ended, the climate became significantly warmer and wetter, no doubt making Obi's jungle much thicker.
Roughly 11,700 years ago, as the most recent ice age ended, the climate became significantly warmer and wetter, no doubt making Obi's jungle much thicker.
While stone takes about twice as long to grind into an axe compared to shell, the harder material keeps its sharp edge for longer.

According to the researchers, it is no coincidence that around this time the first axes crafted from stone rather than sea shells appear, likely in response to their heavy-duty use for clearing and modification of the increasingly dense rainforest.

questions.title: Shipton's team were surprised to find evidence of the Obi islanders' hunting practices.
questions.analysis: 题目要求判断Shipton团队是否对发现Obi岛居民的狩猎实践感到惊讶，而文章中没有直接或是间接的信息表明研究团队对这一发现有任何惊讶的反应。尽管文章描述了狩猎实践的具体内容，比如捕猎Rothschild's cuscus，以及研究团队发现了用于清理丛林有助于狩猎的斧头，但这些信息并不能说明研究者对此感到惊讶。因此，关于他们对狩猎实践的反应的信息处于“未提及”的状态，答案为“NOT GIVEN”。

central_sentences.reason: 未提及Shipton团队是否对狩猎实践感到惊讶。
central_sentences.analysis: 文中仅陈述狩猎事实，未提研究者情绪反应。
central_sentences.paraphrasing: surprised -> 未提及研究者是否惊讶

questions.title: It is thought that the Kelo shelters were occupied continuously until about 1,000 years ago.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“occupied continuously until about 1,000 years ago”与原文中的信息相反。原文提到Kelo shelters在大约8,000年前被遗弃，直到大约1,000年前才重新被占用。因此，题干中的信息是错误的，正确答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.analysis: 8,000年前遗址被弃用，之后到1,000年前无使用证据，非连续居住。
central_sentences.keywords: about 8,000 years ago, abandoned, re-occupied about 1,000 years ago
sentences: The excavations suggest people successfully lived in the two Kelo shelters for about 10,000 years.
But then, about 8,000 years ago, both were abandoned.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: occupied continuously until 1,000 years ago -> both were abandoned; no evidence after this time until about 1,000 years ago

central_sentences.sentence: The excavations suggest people successfully lived in the two Kelo shelters for about 10,000 years.
But then, about 8,000 years ago, both were abandoned.

groups.title: Archaeological findings on Obi
groups.desc:
Questions 8-13

Complete the notes below.
Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
Write your answers in boxes 8-13 on your answer sheet.
groups.collect:
Excavations of rock shelters inside $34086$ near the village of Kelo revealed:
• axes from around 14,000 years ago, probably used to make canoes
• axes made out of $34087$, dating from around 11,700 years ago
• $34088$ of an animal: evidence of what ancient islanders ate
• evidence of travel between islands:
  -  obsidian: a material that is not found naturally on Obi
  -  $34089$ which resembled ones found on other islands.
It is thought that from 8,000 years ago, Obi islanders:
• may have switched from hunting to fishing
• had $34090$ as well as items made out of metal
• probably took part in the production and sale of $34091$.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“rock shelters”等同于原文中的“rock shelters”，“near the village of Kelo”等同于原文中的“Just inland from the village of Kelo”，而“inside”可以对应到“containing”。因此，最合适填入“Excavations of rock shelters inside 【caves】 near the village of Kelo revealed:”。“caves”是原文中提到包含“rock shelters”的地方，因此这个答案是正确的。

central_sentences.sentence: Just inland from the village of Kelo on Obi's northern coast, Shipton and his colleagues found two caves containing prehistoric rock shelters that were suitable for excavation.
central_sentences.translation: 在奥比岛北部海岸，从凯罗村附近内陆，希普顿和他的同事们发现了两个包含史前岩石庇护所的洞穴，非常适合进行挖掘。

target_sen.target_sen: Just inland from the village of Kelo on Obi's northern coast, Shipton and his colleagues found two caves containing prehistoric rock shelters that were suitable for excavation.

syn_ary.synonym: Just inland from the village of Kelo

target_sen.target_sen: Just inland from the village of Kelo on Obi's northern coast, Shipton and his colleagues found two caves containing prehistoric rock shelters that were suitable for excavation.
Just inland from the village of Kelo on Obi's northern coast, Shipton and his colleagues found two caves containing prehistoric rock shelters that were suitable for excavation.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是“stone”，因为文章明确提到在大约11,700年前，斧头是由石头制作的。题干中的“axes made out of”对应原文中的“axes crafted from”，“11,700 years ago”对应“Roughly 11,700 years ago”。因此，“stone”就是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Roughly 11,700 years ago, as the most recent ice age ended, the climate became significantly warmer and wetter, no doubt making Obi's jungle much thicker. According to the researchers, it is no coincidence that around this time the first axes crafted from stone rather than sea shells appear, likely in response to their heavy-duty use for clearing and modification of the increasingly dense rainforest.
central_sentences.translation: 大约11,700年前，最近一次冰河期结束，气候变得明显温暖和潮湿，毫无疑问，使得Obi岛的丛林变得更加茂密。根据研究人员的说法，不久后，首次出现了用石头而不是贝壳制作的斧头，这很可能是为了应对更密集的雨林清理和改造需要。

target_sen.target_sen: According to the researchers, it is no coincidence that around this time the first axes crafted from stone rather than sea shells appear, likely in response to their heavy-duty use for clearing and modification of the increasingly dense rainforest.
Roughly 11,700 years ago, as the most recent ice age ended, the climate became significantly warmer and wetter, no doubt making Obi's jungle much thicker.

questions.analysis: 这个答案是正确的，因为题目要求找到“古代岛民吃什么的证据(evidence of what ancient islanders ate)”。在原文中，"bones"直接作为了开掘出的物品被提及，同时这句话说明了这些骨头是由于猎捕(cuscus)而来的。这里，“bones”作为一个具体证据与题干中的“evidence”相对应，“hunted”作为动作，同题干中的“ate”相对应，表明了这些是古代岛民食物的证据，而且是直接与特定动物(hunted the Rothschild's cuscus)关联的。因此，“bones”既符合是一种“evidence”，也关联到了“what ancient islanders ate”，所以它是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Judging by the bones which the researchers unearthed in the Kelo caves, people living there mainly hunted the Rothschild's cuscus, a possum-like creature that still lives on Obi today.
central_sentences.translation: 根据研究人员在Kelo洞穴发掘出的骨头判断，当地人主要猎捕罗斯柴尔德果狸，一种至今仍生活在奥比岛上的类似负鼠的生物。

target_sen.target_sen: Judging by the bones which the researchers unearthed in the Kelo caves, people living there mainly hunted the Rothschild's cuscus, a possum-like creature that still lives on Obi today.
Judging by the bones which the researchers unearthed in the Kelo caves, people living there mainly hunted the Rothschild's cuscus, a possum-like creature that still lives on Obi today.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“resembled”在原文中对应“similar to”，“other islands”在原文中对应“islands in southern Wallacea”。通过这些同义替换词，可以定位到原文中的句子“...particular types of beads, similar to those previously found on islands in southern Wallacea.”。因此，答案是“beads”，因为它是与其他岛屿上发现的相似的东西。

central_sentences.sentence: It also revealed particular types of beads, similar to those previously found on islands in southern Wallacea.
central_sentences.translation: 它还揭示了特定类型的珠子，这些珠子与之前在南华莱士群岛上发现的珠子相似。

target_sen.target_sen: It also revealed particular types of beads, similar to those previously found on islands in southern Wallacea.
It also revealed particular types of beads, similar to those previously found on islands in southern Wallacea.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“items made out of metal”在原文中对应的是“items made out of gold and silver”。通过这个线索词，我们可以定位到原文中的句子，发现“pottery”是与金属制品并列的另一类物品。因此，正确答案是“pottery”。

central_sentences.sentence: Whatever the reason for the departure, there is no evidence for use of the Kelo shelters after this time, until about 1,000 years ago, when they were re-occupied by people who owned pottery as well as items made out of gold and silver.
central_sentences.translation: 无论离开的原因是什么，在此之后没有证据表明Kelo避难所被使用，直到大约1000年前，当时这些避难所被拥有陶器以及金银制品的人重新占据。

syn_ary.synonym: items made out of gold and silver

target_sen.target_sen: Whatever the reason for the departure, there is no evidence for use of the Kelo shelters after this time, until about 1,000 years ago, when they were re-occupied by people who owned pottery as well as items made out of gold and silver.

questions.analysis: 答案是 "spices"。题干中的 "probably took part in the production and sale of" 与原文中的句子 "It seems likely... involved in the historic trade in spices" 对应。题干问的是参与生产与销售什么，而文中明确提到"Kelo shelters used by people involved in the historic trade in spices"，表示他们参与了香料的交易。因此，答案是 "spices"。

central_sentences.sentence: It seems likely, in view of Obi's location, that this final phase of occupation also saw the Kelo shelters used by people involved in the historic trade in spices between the Maluku islands and the rest of the world.
central_sentences.translation: 鉴于奥比岛的位置，这一最后的居住阶段很可能也见证了凯罗庇护所被参与香料历史贸易的人们使用，这些香料交易发生在马鲁古群岛和世界其他地方之间。

target_sen.target_sen: It seems likely, in view of Obi's location, that this final phase of occupation also saw the Kelo shelters used by people involved in the historic trade in spices between the Maluku islands and the rest of the world.

It seems likely, in view of Obi's location, that this final phase of occupation also saw the Kelo shelters used by people involved in the historic trade in spices between the Maluku islands and the rest of the world.

It seems likely, in view of Obi's location, that this final phase of occupation also saw the Kelo shelters used by people involved in the historic trade in spices between the Maluku islands and the rest of the world.

content.title: The global importance of wetlands
content.summary: {'A': '阐述湿地的定义，指出世界上一半的湿地自 1990 年以来消失或受损，原因包括商业开发等。', 'B': '说明人类历史上围绕湿地发展，湿地对许多社区有重要宗教、历史和考古价值，且很多人依赖湿地农业为生。', 'C': '强调湿地在缓解气候变化方面有关键作用，如作为缓冲保护沿海地区，相关机构正努力恢复湿地森林。', 'D': '指出湿地是有需求商品的丰富来源，如泥炭地被开发会导致碳排放等问题。', 'E': '详细说明泥炭地被开发的过程以及带来的后果，如细菌和真菌分解碳产生二氧化碳等。', 'F': '认为虽然一些产业对湿地的影响明显，但更微妙的影响可能更具破坏性，且人们并非故意破坏湿地。', 'G': '提到要认识到为当地人提供收入的重要性，且这一观点得到相关机构支持。', 'H': '表示如果更多政府认识到湿地的长期价值，湿地的命运会得到改善，有人对此持谨慎乐观态度。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Wetlands are areas where water covers the soil, or is present either at or near the surface of the soil, for all or part of the year. These are complex ecosystems, rich in unique plant and animal life. But according to the World Wide Fund for Nature (WWF), half of the world's wetlands have disappeared since 1990 - converted or destroyed for commercial development, drainage schemes and the extraction of minerals and peat*. Many of those that remain have been damaged by agricultural pesticides and fertilizers, industrial pollutants, and construction works.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Throughout history, humans have gathered around wetlands, and their fertile ecosystems have played an important part in human development. Consequently, they are of considerable religious, historical and archaeological value to many communities around the world. "Wetlands directly support the livelihoods and well-being of millions of people," says Dr Matthew McCartney, principal researcher and hydrologist at the International Water Management Institute (IWMI). "In many developing countries, large numbers of people are dependent on wetland agriculture for their livelihoods."

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: They also serve a crucial environmental purpose. "Wetlands are one of the key tools in mitigating climate change across the planet," says Pieter van Eijk, head of Climate Adaptation at Wetlands International (WI), pointing to their use as buffers that protect coastal areas from sea-level rise and extreme weather events such as hurricanes and flooding. Wetland coastal forests provide food and water, as well as shelter from storms, and WI and other agencies are working to restore those forests which have been lost. "It can be as simple as planting a few trees per hectare to create shade and substantially change a microclimate," he says. "Implementing climate change projects isn't so much about money."

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: The world's wetlands are, unfortunately, rich sources for in-demand commodities, such as palm oil and pulpwood. Peatlands - wetlands with a waterlogged organic soil layer - are particularly targeted. When peatlands are drained for cultivation, they become net carbon emitters instead of active carbon stores, and, according to Marcel Silvius, head of Climate-smart Land-use at WI, this practice causes six per cent of all global carbon emissions. The clearance of peatlands for planting also increases the risk of forest fires, which release huge amounts of CO2. "We're seeing huge peatland forests with extremely high biodiversity value being lost for a few decades of oil palm revenues," says Silvius.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: The damage starts when logging companies arrive to clear the trees. They dig ditches to enter the peat swamps by boat and then float the logs out the same way. These are then used to drain water out of the peatlands to allow for the planting of corn, oil palms or pulpwood trees. Once the water has drained away, bacteria and fungi then break down the carbon in the peat and turn it into CO2 and methane. Meanwhile, the remainder of the solid matter in the peat starts to move downwards, in a process known as subsidence**. Peat comprises 90 per cent water, so this is one of the most alarming consequences of peatland clearances. "In the tropics, peat subsides at about four centimetres a year, so within half a century, very large landscapes on Sumatra and Borneo will become flooded as the peat drops below water level," says Silvius. "It's a huge catastrophe that's in preparation. Some provinces will lose 40 per cent of their landmass."

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: And while these industries affect wetlands in ways that can easily be documented, Dr Dave Tickner of the WWF believes that more subtle impacts can be even more devastating. "Sediment run-off and fertilizers can be pretty invisible," says Tickner. "Over-extraction of water is equally invisible. You do get shock stories about rivers running red, or even catching fire, but there's seldom one big impact that really hurts a wetland." Tickner does not blame anyone for deliberate damage, however. "I've worked on wetland issues for 20 years and have never met anybody who wanted to damage a wetland," he says. "It isn't something that people generally set out to do. Quite often, the effects simply come from people trying to make a living."

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Silvius also acknowledges the importance of income generation. "It's not that we just want to restore the biodiversity of wetlands - which we do - but we recognise there's a need to provide an income for local people." This approach is supported by IWMI. "The idea is that people in a developing country will only protect wetlands if they value and profit from them," says McCartney. "For sustainability, it's essential that local people are involved in wetland planning and decision making and have clear rights to use wetlands."

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text:
The fortunes of wetlands would be improved, Silvius suggests, if more governments recognised their long-term value. "Different governments have different attitudes," he says, and goes on to explain that some countries place a high priority on restoring wetlands, while others still deny the issue. McCartney is cautiously optimistic, however. "Awareness of the importance of wetlands is growing," he says. "It's true that wetland degradation still continues at a rapid pace, but my impression is that things are slowly changing." 
 * peat: a brown deposit formed by the partial decomposition of vegetation in wet acidic conditions, often cut out and dried for use as fuel 
 ** subsidence: the sinking of the Earth's surface, most often caused by the removal of resources from the ground

Clearing peatland forests to make way for oil palm plantations destroys the $34098$ of the local environment.

questions.analysis: 这一题的答案是 "carbon"。题目问的是被排水的泥炭地开始释放什么而不是储存什么。线索词 "drained" 在原文中找到了对应的 "drained for cultivation"，"release" 和 "storing" 则对应 "emitters" 和 "stores"。通过理解这句话的意思可以得知，泥炭地被排水后开始释放 "carbon" 而不是储存它。因此答案为 "carbon"。

central_sentences.translation: 当泥炭地被排水用于耕作时，它们成为净碳排放源而不是活跃的碳储存体...

Dave Tickner。题干中的 "People cause harm to wetlands without having any intention to do so" 意思是人们在无意中对湿地造成了伤害。文章中 Dr Dave Tickner 的话 "I've worked on wetland issues for 20 years and have never met anybody who wanted to damage a wetland" 和 "It isn't something that people generally set out to do" 直接对应了题干的意思，说明人们并非有意去破坏湿地，而是无意中造成了伤害。

questions.title: Initiatives to reverse environmental damage need not be complex.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“Initiatives to reverse environmental damage need not be complex”意思是“逆转环境破坏的措施不需要复杂”。在原文中，Pieter van Eijk提到“通过每公顷种植几棵树来创造阴影并显著改变小气候，这可以很简单”，这与题干的意思相符。因此，正确答案是B: Pieter van Eijk。

central_sentences.sentence: "It can be as simple as planting a few trees per hectare to create shade and substantially change a microclimate," he says."Implementing climate change projects isn't so much about money."
central_sentences.translation: “这可以像每公顷种植几棵树来创造阴影并显著改变小气候一样简单，”他说。“实施气候变化项目并不完全是关于金钱的问题。”

target_sen.target_sen: "Implementing climate change projects isn't so much about money."

"Wetlands are one of the key tools in mitigating climate change across the planet," says Pieter van Eijk, head of Climate Adaptation at Wetlands International (WI), pointing to their use as buffers that protect coastal areas from sea-level rise and extreme weather events such as hurricanes and flooding.
"It can be as simple as planting a few trees per hectare to create shade and substantially change a microclimate," he says.

content.title: Is the era of artificial speech translation upon us?

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Noise, Alex Waibel tells me, is one of the major challenges that artificial speech translation has to meet. A device may be able to recognise speech in a laboratory, or a meeting room, but will struggle to cope with the kind of background noise I can hear in my office surrounding Professor Waibel as he speaks to me from Kyoto station in Japan. I'm struggling to follow him in English, on a scratchy line that reminds me we are nearly 10,000 kilometres apart—and that distance is still an obstacle to communication even if you're speaking the same language, as we are. We haven't reached the future yet. If we had, Waibel would have been able to speak more comfortably in his native German and I would have been able to hear his words in English.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: At Karlsruhe Institute of Technology, where he is a professor of computer science, Waibel and his colleagues already give lectures in German that their students can follow in English via an electronic translator. The system generates text that students can read on their laptops or phones, so the process is somewhat similar to subtitling. It helps that lecturers speak clearly, don't have to compete with background chatter, and say much the same thing each year.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: The idea of artificial speech translation has been around for a long time. Douglas Adams' science fiction novel, The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy, published in 1979, featured a life form called the "Babel fish" which, when placed in the ear, enabled a listener to understand any language in the universe. It came to represent one of those devices that technology enthusiasts dream of long before they become practically realisable, like TVs flat enough to hang on walls: objects that we once could only dream of having but that are now commonplace. Now devices that look like prototype Babel fish have started to appear, riding a wave of advances in artificial translation and voice recognition.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: At this stage, however, they seem to be regarded as eye-catching novelties rather than steps towards what Waibel calls "making a language-transparent society". They tend to be domestic devices or applications suitable for hotel check-ins, for example, providing a practical alternative to speaking traveller's English. The efficiency of the translator is less important than the social function. However, "Professionals are less inclined to be patient in a conversation," founder and CEO at Waverly Labs, Andrew Ochoa, observes. To redress this, Waverly is now preparing a new model for professional applications, which entails performance improvements in speech recognition, translation accuracy and the time it takes to deliver the translated speech.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: For a conversation, both speakers need to have devices called Pilots (translator earpieces) in their ears. "We find that there's a barrier with sharing one of the earphones with a stranger," says Ochoa. That can't have been totally unexpected. The problem would be solved if earpiece translators became sufficiently prevalent that strangers would be likely to already have their own in their ears. Whether that happens, and how quickly, will probably depend not so much on the earpieces themselves, but on the prevalence of voice-controlled devices and artificial translation in general.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Waibel highlights the significance of certain Asian nations, noting that voice translation has really taken off in countries such as Japan with a range of systems. There is still a long way to go, though. A translation system needs to be simultaneous, like the translator's voice speaking over the foreign politician being interviewed on the TV, rather than in sections that oblige speakers to pause after every few remarks and wait for the translation to be delivered. It needs to work offline, for situations where internet access isn't possible, and to address apprehensions about the amount of private speech data accumulating in the cloud, having been sent to servers for processing.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Systems not only need to cope with physical challenges such as noise, they will also need to be socially aware by addressing people in the right way. Some cultural traditions demand solemn respect for academic status, for example, and it is only polite to respect this. Etiquette-sensitive artificial translators could relieve people of the need to know these differing cultural norms. At the same time, they might help to preserve local customs, slowing the spread of habits associated with international English, such as its readiness to get on first-name terms.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: Professors and other professionals will not outsource language awareness to software, though. If the technology matures into seamless, ubiquitous artificial speech translation, it will actually add value to language skills. Whether it will help people conduct their family lives or relationships is open to question—though one noteworthy possibility is that it could overcome the language barriers that often arise between generations after migration, leaving children and their grandparents without a shared language.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: Whatever uses it is put to, though, it will never be as good as the real thing. Even if voice-morphing technology simulates the speaker's voice, their lip movements won't match, and they will look like they are in a dubbed movie. The contrast will underline the value of shared languages, and the value of learning them. Sharing a language can promote a sense of belonging and community, as with the international scientists who use English as a lingua franca, where their predecessors used Latin. Though the practical need for a common language will diminish, the social value of sharing one will persist. And software will never be a substitute for the subtle but vital understanding that comes with knowledge of a language.

groups.desc:
Questions 27-30

Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
Write the correct letter in boxes 27-30 on your answer sheet.

questions.title: What does the reader learn about the conversation in the first paragraph?

options.text: The speakers are communicating in different languages.
Neither of the speakers is familiar with their environment.
The topic of the conversation is difficult for both speakers.
Aspects of the conversation are challenging for both speakers.

questions.analysis: 答案是D。文章中提到噪音是挑战，并且作者在努力跟上对话，说明对话中的某些方面对双方来说都是有挑战的。这些都符合D选项的意思。

central_sentences.sentence: Noise, Alex Waibel tells me, is one of the major challenges that artificial speech translation has to meet. A device may be able to recognise speech in a laboratory, or a meeting room, but will struggle to cope with the kind of background noise I can hear in my office surrounding Professor Waibel as he speaks to me from Kyoto station in Japan. I"m struggling to follow him in English, on a scratchy line that reminds me we are nearly 10,000 kilometres apart - and that distance is still an obstacle to communication even if you're speaking the same language, as we are. We
central_sentences.translation: Alex Waibel 告诉我，噪音是人工语音翻译必须应对的主要挑战之一。一台设备可能在实验室或会议室能够识别语音，但在我所在办公室周围日本京都站听到的背景噪音中，设备会很难应对。我在努力跟上他的英语，用一个嘈杂的电话线，这提醒我我们相隔近10000公里——即使我们讲的是同一语言，距离仍然是交流的障碍。

questions.title: What assists the electronic translator during lectures at Karlsruhe Institute of Technology?
questions.analysis: 在卡尔斯鲁厄理工学院，讲师们的演讲内容年复一年较为相同，这种重复性的内容帮助了电子翻译系统的准确翻译，因为预测性强。这是文章中直接对应选项A的情况，因此选项A是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: It helps that lecturers speak clearly, don"t have to compete with background chatter, and say much the same thing each year
central_sentences.translation: 讲师讲话清晰、不必与背景噪音竞争、并且每年讲的内容大致相同，这些都有助于翻译系统的运作。

target_sen.target_sen: It helps that lecturers speak clearly, don"t have to compete with background chatter, and say much the same thing each year.

At Karlsruhe Institute of Technology, where he is a professor of computer science, Waibel and his colleagues already give lectures in German that their students can follow in English via an electronic translator.
The system generates text that students can read on their laptops or phones, so the process is somewhat similar to subtitling.

At Karlsruhe Institute of Technology, where he is a professor of computer science, Waibel and his colleagues already give lectures in German that their students can follow in English via an electronic translator.

questions.title: When referring to The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy, the writer suggests that

options.text: the Babel fish was considered undesirable at the time.
this book was not seriously intending to predict the future.
artificial speech translation was not a surprising development.
some speech translation techniques are better than others.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是C，因为文章明确提到“人工语音翻译的概念已经存在很长时间了”，这表明人工语音翻译的发展并不令人惊讶。题目中的线索词“artificial speech translation”和“not a surprising development”在原文中有直接对应的同义替换词。

central_sentences.sentence: The idea of artificial speech translation has been around for a long time.
central_sentences.translation: 人工语音翻译的概念已经存在很长时间了。

questions.title: What does the writer say about sharing earpieces?

options.text: It is something people will get used to doing.
The reluctance to do this is understandable.
The equipment will be unnecessary in the future.
It is something few people need to worry about.

questions.analysis: 学生在题干和文章中找到相同或近似的表达之后，可以确定对应关系。题干中的“sharing earpieces”在文章中对应的是“sharing one of the earphones”。文章中提到“there's a barrier with sharing one of the earphones with a stranger”和“can't have been totally unexpected”明确表达了对这种情绪的理解，这与选项B中的“understandable”（可以理解的）是一致的，因此正确答案是B。

central_sentences.sentence: We find that there's a barrier with sharing one of the earphones with a stranger," says Ochoa. That can"t have been totally unexpected. The
central_sentences.translation: “我们发现，与陌生人共享一个耳机是有障碍的，”Ochoa说。这并不完全出乎意料。

target_sen.target_sen: "We find that there's a barrier with sharing one of the earphones with a stranger," says Ochoa.

groups.desc:
Questions 31-34

Complete each sentence with the correct ending, A-F, below.

Write the correct letter, A-F, in boxes 31-34 on your answer sheet.

list.text: but there are concerns about this.
as systems do not need to conform to standard practices.
but they are far from perfect.
and have an awareness of good manners.

questions.title: Speech translation methods are developing fast in Japan
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "developing fast in Japan" 对应原文中的 "has really taken off in countries such as Japan"，表示日本的语音翻译技术发展迅速。而 "There is still a long way to go, though" 则表示尽管如此，技术仍然不完美。因此，正确答案是 C: but they are far from perfect。

central_sentences.sentence: There is still a long way to go, though.
central_sentences.translation: 不过，仍然有很长的路要走。

target_sen.target_sen: Waibel highlights the significance of certain Asian nations, noting that voice translation has really taken off in countries such as Japan with a range of systems.
Waibel highlights the significance of certain Asian nations, noting that voice translation has really taken off in countries such as Japan with a range of systems.

questions.title: TV interviews that use translation voiceover methods are successful
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "TV interviews" 和 "translation voiceover methods" 在原文中对应 "interviewed on the TV" 和 "translator's voice speaking over"。原文提到翻译系统需要是同步的，这样才能成功进行电视采访。因此，正确答案是 E: because translation is immediate。

central_sentences.sentence: A translation system needs to be simultaneous, like the translator's voice speaking over the foreign politician being interviewed on the TV, rather than in sections that oblige speakers to pause after every few remarks and wait for the translation to be delivered.
central_sentences.translation: 翻译系统需要是同步的，就像翻译者的声音覆盖在电视上被采访的外国政治家身上一样，而不是分段进行，让说话者在每几句话后暂停等待翻译。

target_sen.target_sen: A translation system needs to be simultaneous, like the translator's voice speaking over the foreign politician being interviewed on the TV, rather than in sections that oblige speakers to pause after every few remarks and wait for the translation to be delivered.

A translation system needs to be simultaneous, like the translator's voice speaking over the foreign politician being interviewed on the TV, rather than in sections that oblige speakers to pause after every few remarks and wait for the translation to be delivered.

A translation system needs to be simultaneous, like the translator's voice speaking over the foreign politician being interviewed on the TV, rather than in sections that oblige speakers to pause after every few remarks and wait for the translation to be delivered.

A translation system needs to be simultaneous, like the translator's voice speaking over the foreign politician being interviewed on the TV, rather than in sections that oblige speakers to pause after every few remarks and wait for the translation to be delivered.

questions.title: Future translation systems should address people appropriately
questions.analysis: 正确答案F中的“有礼貌的意识”与原文中提到的“正确的方式(address people in the right way)”是同义的，指代系统应该具备的社交意识，因此选项F符合原文的描述。该句子指出了翻译系统需要不仅要应对噪声等物理挑战，也要理解并尊重社会和文化上的不同，正确地与人交流，这与选项F强调的礼貌意识相吻合。

central_sentences.sentence: Systems not only need to cope with physical challenges such as noise, they will also need to be socially aware by addressing people in the right way.
central_sentences.translation: 系统不仅需要应对噪音等物理挑战，还需要在社会意识上通过正确的方式对待他人。

target_sen.target_sen: Systems not only need to cope with physical challenges such as noise, they will also need to be socially aware by addressing people in the right way.
Systems not only need to cope with physical challenges such as noise, they will also need to be socially aware by addressing people in the right way.

questions.title: Users may be able to maintain their local customs
questions.analysis: 正确答案是 B: as systems do not need to conform to standard practices. 文章中提到“etiquette-sensitive artificial translators could relieve people of the need to know these differing cultural norms”，这意味着系统不需要遵循标准做法，从而帮助用户保持本地习俗。

central_sentences.sentence: Etiquette-sensitive artificial translators could relieve people of the need to know these differing cultural norms. At the same time, they might help to preserve local customs, slowing the spread of habits associated with international English, such as its readiness to get on first-name terms.
central_sentences.translation: 对礼仪敏感的人工翻译器可以让人们不需要了解这些不同的文化规范。同时，它们可能有助于保持本地习俗，减缓与国际英语相关习惯的传播，例如直接称呼名字的习惯。

target_sen.target_sen: At the same time, they might help to preserve local customs, slowing the spread of habits associated with international English, such as its readiness to get on first-name terms.

groups.desc:
Questions 35-40

Do the following statements agree with the views of the writer in Reading Passage 3?

In boxes 35-40 on your answer sheet, write

YES if the statement agrees with the views of the writer
NO if the statement contradicts the views of the writer
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

questions.title: Language translation systems will be seen as very useful throughout the academic and professional worlds.
questions.analysis: 该题的正确答案是NO，因为尽管文章提到了一些翻译系统在学术和专业应用中的实际例子，但文章中也指出了这些系统存在的缺陷和挑战。例如，在“academic and professional worlds”（学术和专业领域），系统遇到了一些问题，如社交障碍、物理挑战和文化尊重等问题。因此，不能认为这些系统在学术和专业领域被广泛认为是“very useful”（非常有用）的。

central_sentences.sentence: For a conversation, both speakers need to have devices called Pilots (translator earpieces) in their ears. "We find that there's a barrier with sharing one of the earphones with a stranger," says Ochoa. That can"t have been totally unexpected. The problem would be solved if earpiece translators became sufficiently prevalent that strangers would be likely to already have their own in their ears. Whether that happens, and how quickly, will probably depend not so much on the earpieces themselves, but on the prevalence of voice-controlled devices and artificial translation in general
central_sentences.translation: 在对话中，双方都需要在耳朵里佩戴一种叫做Pilots（翻译耳机）的设备。“我们发现与陌生人共用一个耳机是个障碍，”Ochoa说。这点不太意外。如果耳机式翻译器普及到足以让陌生人也可能已经拥有自己的耳机，这个问题就会得到解决。这个过程能否发生，以及速度如何，可能更多的取决于语音控制设备和人工翻译技术的普及程度，而不仅仅是耳机本身。

central_sentences.sentence: Systems not only need to cope with physical challenges such as noise, they will also need to be socially aware by addressing people in the right way. Some cultural traditions demand solemn respect for academic status, for example, and it is only polite to respect this.
central_sentences.translation: 系统不仅需要应对诸如噪音等物理挑战，还需要具备社会意识，以正确的方式与人交往。例如，一些文化传统要求对学术地位保持庄重的尊重，尊重这点是应该的。

target_sen.target_sen: For a conversation, both speakers need to have devices called Pilots (translator earpieces) in their ears.
The problem would be solved if earpiece translators became sufficiently prevalent that strangers would be likely to already have their own in their ears.
Etiquette-sensitive artificial translators could relieve people of the need to know these differing cultural norms.

questions.title: The overall value of automated translation to family life is yet to be shown.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词 "overall value" 对应于原文中的 "open to question"，"family life" 对应于 "family lives or relationships"，而 "yet to be shown" 也同样对应 "open to question"。原文提到自动翻译对家庭生活（family life）的影响是否有帮助仍然是不确定的（open to question），这与题干表达的意思完全一致。因此，这道题的答案是 YES，表示题干信息在文章中明确提到过。

central_sentences.sentence: Whether it will help people conduct their family lives or relationships is open to question - though one noteworthy possibility is that it could overcome the language barriers that often arise between generations after migration, leaving children and their grandparents without a shared language.
central_sentences.translation: 它是否能帮助人们进行家庭生活或关系尚未可知——尽管有一种值得注意的可能性是，它可以克服通常在移民后代和祖辈之间产生的语言障碍，使孩子和祖父母之间无共享语言的情况得到缓解。

target_sen.target_sen: Whether it will help people conduct their family lives or relationships is open to question - though one noteworthy possibility is that it could overcome the language barriers that often arise between generations after migration, leaving children and their grandparents without a shared language.

Whether it will help people conduct their family lives or relationships is open to question - though one noteworthy possibility is that it could overcome the language barriers that often arise between generations after migration, leaving children and their grandparents without a shared language.

Whether it will help people conduct their family lives or relationships is open to question - though one noteworthy possibility is that it could overcome the language barriers that often arise between generations after migration, leaving children and their grandparents without a shared language.

questions.title: Automated translation could make life more difficult for immigrant families.
questions.analysis: 文章中并没有明确提到自动翻译技术会使移民家庭的生活更加困难。相反，它提出了自动化翻译可能克服代际间的语言障碍这一积极的可能性。因此，题干中的陈述与文中信息不符，所以答案是NO。

central_sentences.sentence: Whether it will help people conduct their family lives or relationships is open to question - though one noteworthy possibility is that it could overcome the language barriers that often arise between generations after migration, leaving children and their grandparents without a shared language.
central_sentences.translation: 它是否会帮助人们处理家庭生活或关系尚有疑问——尽管有一个值得注意的可能性是，它能克服移民后不同代际间经常出现的语言障碍，这种障碍使得儿童和他们的祖父母没有共同的语言。

target_sen.target_sen: If the technology matures into seamless, ubiquitous artificial speech translation, it will actually add value to language skills.

Whether it will help people conduct their family lives or relationships is open to question - though one noteworthy possibility is that it could overcome the language barriers that often arise between generations after migration, leaving children and their grandparents without a shared language.

Whether it will help people conduct their family lives or relationships is open to question - though one noteworthy possibility is that it could overcome the language barriers that often arise between generations after migration, leaving children and their grandparents without a shared language.

questions.title: Visual aspects of language translation are being considered by scientists.
questions.analysis: 题目询问的是科学家是否正在考虑语言翻译的视觉方面（Visual aspects of language translation），但在文章中没有找到与 ‘Visual aspects’ 相关的原文内容或讨论，也没有相关的同义替换词出现。因此，对于这个信息，文章没有给出，答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.reason: 提到视觉问题，但未表述“scientists are considering”，信息不足，NOT GIVEN。
central_sentences.analysis: 只说会口型不匹配，未说科学家在研究。
sentences: Even if voice-morphing technology simulates the speaker's voice, their lip movements won't match, and they will look like they are in a dubbed movie.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: visual aspects considered -> lip movements won't match (提到但非科学家在研究)

central_sentences.sentence: Even if voice-morphing technology simulates the speaker's voice, their lip movements won't match, and they will look like they are in a dubbed movie.

questions.title: International scientists have found English easier to translate into other languages than Latin.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息“International scientists have found English easier to translate into other languages than Latin”在原文中没有明确提到。虽然文章提到国际科学家使用英语作为通用语，但并没有提到英语比拉丁语更容易翻译。因此，正确答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: Sharing a language can promote a sense of belonging and community, as with the international scientists who use English as a lingua franca, where their predecessors used Latin.
central_sentences.translation: 共享一种语言可以促进归属感和社区感，就像使用英语作为通用语的国际科学家一样，他们的前辈使用拉丁语。

target_sen.target_sen: Sharing a language can promote a sense of belonging and community, as with the international scientists who use English as a lingua franca, where their predecessors used Latin.
Sharing a language can promote a sense of belonging and community, as with the international scientists who use English as a lingua franca, where their predecessors used Latin.

questions.title: As far as language is concerned, there is a difference between people's social and practical needs.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“language”在原文中直接提到，“social needs”对应“social function”和“social value”，“practical needs”对应“practical need”和“efficiency”。原文明确指出了翻译器的效率（实用需求）和社交功能（社会需求）之间的区别，并且提到尽管实际需求会减少，但社会价值会持续存在。这些信息表明题干陈述是正确的，因此答案是 YES。

central_sentences.sentence: The efficiency of the translator is less important than the social function. However, "Professionals are less inclined to be patient in a conversation," founder and CEO at Waverly Labs, Andrew Ochoa, observes.
central_sentences.translation: 翻译器的效率不如其社交功能重要。然而，Waverly Labs的创始人兼CEO Andrew Ochoa 观察到，“专业人士在对话中不太愿意耐心等待。”

central_sentences.sentence: Though the practical need for a common language will diminish, the social value of sharing one will persist.
central_sentences.translation: 尽管对共同语言的实际需求会减少，但共享语言的社会价值将持续存在。

target_sen.target_sen: At this stage, however, they seem to be regarded as eye-catching novelties rather than steps towards what Waibel calls "making a language-transparent society".
The contrast will underline the value of shared languages, and the value of learning them.
Sharing a language can promote a sense of belonging and community, as with the international scientists who use English as a lingua franca, where their predecessors used Latin.
Though the practical need for a common language will diminish, the social value of sharing one will persist.
And software will never be a substitute for the subtle but vital understanding that comes with knowledge of a language.
Though the practical need for a common language will diminish, the social value of sharing one will persist.
The efficiency of the translator is less important than the social function.
